name: Telecom & Mobile official sources
notes: Telecom is the most price-perishable category on the site. Operator tariffs, roaming rates, pay-TV packages and SMS gateway pricing all move faster than the review cadence, and NGN/GHS/ETB inflation compounds it.

data/telecom/country-telecom-index.js carries a single lastUpdated stamp for all 12 markets. That is a floor, not a per-figure date: a market can be re-checked without the stamp moving, and the stamp can move without every market being re-checked. Treat any price older than highRiskCadenceDays as planning-grade.

SIM registration and number portability encode legal deadlines and penalties. Per the government rule, that class of claim must never be published without an official-source link. Those two datasets are the highest-risk entries in this ledger and currently have the weakest source coverage.
Never update a fee, tariff, deadline or penalty because a URL's content hash changed. Open the page and read it, exactly as .claude/rules/government.md requires.

Several sources below are commercial or aggregator pages, not regulators. They are recorded as sourceType secondary-aggregator or vendor-pricing so they are never mistaken for authority. A regulator source always outranks them.

sources.title: Mobile Economy, Sub-Saharan Africa
sources.notes: Context and subscriber scale only. Does not evidence any per-operator price in the dataset.

sources.title: WhatsApp Business Platform pricing
sources.notes: Authoritative for WhatsApp conversation rates. Only NG, KE, ZA, GH carry whatsappBusiness data; the other 8 markets are unpriced and the tool now filters them out rather than rendering them as free.

sources.title: Starlink availability map
sources.notes: HIGH PRIORITY. The dataset's starlinkAvailable flags are stale by inspection - several markets are marked false that have had commercial service for some time. Verify every flag against this map before the next release. Pricing exists for NG, KE, ZA only.

sources.title: DStv Nigeria package comparison
sources.notes: Pay-TV reprices at least annually and DStv Nigeria has repriced since the dataset stamp. Only the NG storefront is bound here; KE, ZA and GH package prices have no source.

sources.title: Twilio SMS pricing
sources.notes: The linked page is the US price list. It does not evidence African per-SMS rates. Replace with the per-country Twilio pricing page before treating bulkSMS as sourced.

sources.title: Africa's Talking SMS documentation
sources.notes: Documentation collection, not a rate card. Locate the per-country pricing page to evidence the rates.

sources.title: Africa eSIM plans
sources.notes: Supports the eSIM guidance in the roaming tips only. Does not evidence any operator roaming rate.

sources.title: Worldwide mobile data pricing index
sources.notes: Third-party aggregator, not an operator or regulator. Acceptable for a cross-market sanity check on avgDataCostPerGB; not acceptable as the basis of a published price. Never cite as official.

sources.title: Numbering and number portability
sources.notes: Regulator source. Outranks any vendor page for South African portability rules.

sources.title: CA Kenya document repository
sources.notes: Deep link to a single repository item. Verify it still resolves and still covers the claim before relying on it; repository item URLs are fragile.

sources.title: SIM registration process
sources.notes: Regulator source for Ghana SIM registration.

gaps.notes: Recorded deliberately. These are claims the site publishes with no source bound to them. A gap is a known liability, not an oversight - do not delete an entry here without either finding the source or removing the claim.

unsourcedClaims.why: Every bundle price across all 12 markets is hardcoded with no per-operator source. The page's own FAQ previously claimed prices were 'sourced from official operator websites and updated regularly', which the codebase could not support; that copy has been corrected to state the real review date.
Voice/SMS/data roaming rates for NG, KE, ZA, GH have no operator source. The other 8 markets have no roaming data at all and are now filtered out of the home-country selector.
ISP speeds and prices for 4 of 12 markets, unsourced and undated.
Stated as fact in the meta description. This is an informal secondary-market rate with no regulator and no source. Either attribute it or soften it to a modelled assumption.
Tiers (5/10/15/25%) are hardcoded inline in the page with no provider attribution.
Undated. Low perishability, but RW and ET are missing entirely despite both appearing in operators.

manualReviewRules: Blocked or unreachable regulator portals go to a manual-review queue. Do not infer that a rule is unchanged because its page did not load.
A vendor or aggregator page never overrides a regulator page for the same claim.
SIM-registration deadlines and portability penalties require a regulator source. If there is no regulator URL for a market, the page must not state the deadline as fact.
If a price is older than highRiskCadenceDays, the surface that renders it must show its review date rather than presenting it as current.
